creator: swhetzel@gmail.com
text:
On 1/7/08, Mikhail T. <mi@aldan.algebra.com> wrote:
> Hello!
>
> The attached patch updates the port to 5.20e, which is available for
> both FreeBSD-4/5 and, separately, FreeBSD-6.x
>
> It should allow the port to survive, along with the amavis-ports, which
> depend on it and are currently also due to be dropped due to the current
> version of vscan depending on compat3x.
>
> It all seems to work, except for one remaining wrinkle -- getting this
> particular 32-bit binary to run on my amd64-machine required a nasty
> hack of /etc/libmap32.conf (attached). I'm still figuring out, whether
> it is just my box, or whether all amd64 installations will have to do
> this.
>
> It also needs to be tested on a machine running 5.x or 4.x...

It only needs to be tested on 5.x, as the ports collection doesn't
support 4.x due to it's End of Life status.

>
> Other highlights:
>         . Don't fetch the updated definitions during install
>         . Use SUB_FILES mechanism instead of sed-ing the files
>           inside the port
>         . When fetching updated definitions be a bit more
>           verbose, when a need to update is detected -- the
>           script is still silent, when the data is up-to-date,
>           this allowing easy use inside a cronjob
>         . When fetching updated definitions, use dat-VER.zip instead
>           of tar -- zip-files are about 15% smaller, which is well
>           worth the resulting RUN_DEPENDency on unzip
>
> The up-to-date version of the patch is:
>
>         http://aldan.algebra.com/~mi/vscan-update.diff
>
> Scot, I don't think, this port will be very successful without a
> mainainer. Would you like to become one?
>
That would be fine to make me the maintainer of the port.

After patching, installing uvscan, and running update_dat, uvscan
fails due to missing license.dat and messages.dat files:

hp010# ls /usr/local/libexec/uvscan
contact.txt     license.txt     signlic.txt
license.dat     messages.dat    uvscan
hp010# /usr/local/sbin/update_dat
dat-5201.zip                                  100% of   16 MB   31 kBps 00m00s
Archive:  dat-5201.zip
  inflating: clean.dat               [binary]
  inflating: names.dat               [binary]
  inflating: pkgdesc.ini             [binary]
  inflating: scan.dat                [binary]
  inflating: internet.dat            [binary]
Mon Jan  7 23:27:40 CST 2008 Successfully updated AntiVirus DAT files to 5201
hp010# ls /usr/local/libexec/uvscan
clean.dat       internet.dat    names.dat       scan.dat        uvscan
contact.txt     license.txt     pkgdesc.ini     signlic.txt
hp010# uvscan --version
Missing or invalid "/usr/local/libexec/uvscan/messages.dat"

The problem is that the zip file doesn't contain these files, but
update_dat is using mv to move all the *.dat files to *.dat.bak.  The
fix is to make the following changes:

--- update_dat.in       2008-01-07 23:00:46.975095998 -0600
+++ update_dat.in-new   2008-01-07 23:15:51.401341506 -0600
@@ -17,16 +17,16 @@

 # Check to make sure the requested version is "newer" then the current
 if [ "$cur_AVVER" -lt "$AVVER" ]; then
-       for i in *.tar ; do
+       for i in *.zip ; do
                [ -f "$i" ] && mv $i $i.old
        done
        fetch http://download.nai.com/products/datfiles/4.x/nai/dat-$AVVER.zip
|| exit 2
        for i in *.dat ; do
-               [ -f "$i" ] && mv $i $i.bak
+               [ -f "$i" ] && cp -p $i $i.bak
        done
        unzip -xa dat-$AVVER.zip '*.ini' '*.dat' || exit 3
        tr -d '\r' < pkgdesc.ini > pkgdesc.ini.new && mv
pkgdesc.ini.new pkgdesc.ini
-       rm -f *.bak dat-$AVVER.zip *.exe *.old
+       rm -f *.bak *.old
        chmod 444 *.dat
        echo "`date` Successfully updated AntiVirus DAT files to $AVVER"
 fi


hp010# make deinstall
===>  Deinstalling for security/vscan
===>   Deinstalling uvscan-5.20e
pkg_delete: file '/usr/local/libexec/uvscan/license.dat' doesn't exist
pkg_delete: file '/usr/local/libexec/uvscan/messages.dat' doesn't exist
pkg_delete: unable to completely remove directory '/usr/local/libexec/uvscan'
pkg_delete: couldn't entirely delete package (perhaps the packing list is
incorrectly specified?)
hp010# make install
:
hp010#  uvscan --version
Virus Scan for BSD v5.20.0
Copyright (c) 1992-2007 McAfee, Inc. All rights reserved.
(408) 988-3832  EVALUATION COPY - Apr 24 2007

Scan engine v5.2.00 for BSD.
Virus data file v5201 created Jan 07 2008
Scanning for 362495 viruses, trojans and variants.

I tested uvscan using the EICAR test file, and it is able to detect it properly.

hp010# uvscan eicar.test
/usr/ports/security/vscan/eicar.test
        Found: EICAR test file NOT a virus.


I tested the port on:

hp010# uname -a
FreeBSD hp010.hetzel.org 8.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 8.0-CURRENT #0: Mon Jan
7 05:36:18 CST 2008
root@hp010.hetzel.org:/usr/src/8x/sys/amd64/compile/DV8135NR  amd64

and with an empty libmap32.conf.

Scot
